qa_pairs.question: What does the "Queued jobs" metric represent in the SLURM usage figure legend, and how is it displayed?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Queued jobs" metric in the SLURM usage figure legend represents the quantity of resources belonging to jobs that are pending in the job queue. It is displayed with narrow gray bars for each day in the figure window.

qa_pairs.question: What information does the "Total" option in the SLURM usage figure legend provide?
qa_pairs.answer: The "Total" option in the SLURM usage figure legend adds text to the top of each bar, indicating the daily total of the metric across all users for that specific day.

qa_pairs.question: How do users control the visibility of individual or multiple items within the SLURM usage figure legend?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can control the visibility of items in the SLURM usage figure legend by single-clicking an item to toggle its individual presence in the figure. To toggle all other items on or off simultaneously, users can double-click any item in the legend.
